title: Fulbright U.S. Student Program: Open Study/Research Award in Sri Lanka
description: A nine-month grant for U.S. citizens to independently design and pursue a research project in Sri Lanka in nearly any field of study (clinical medical research excluded). Grantees receive round-trip international airfare, a monthly living stipend paid in Sri Lankan rupees, and health benefits, administered jointly by the U.S.-Sri Lanka Fulbright Commission and the U.S. Department of State.
org: Fulbright U.S. Student Program / U.S.-Sri Lanka Fulbright Commission
visaNote: Grantees obtain a Sri Lankan entry/research visa with support from the Fulbright Commission.

title: Taiwan Scholarship (MOE) for Degree Study in Taiwan
description: Government-funded scholarship enabling international students to pursue undergraduate, master's, or doctoral degree programs at universities in Taiwan. It covers tuition/fees (up to a per-semester cap) and provides a monthly stipend of NT$15,000 (undergraduate) or NT$20,000 (graduate); applications are submitted annually through the Taiwan embassy or representative office in the applicant's home country, typically in a February-March window.
org: Ministry of Education, Taiwan (ROC), with Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Taiwan (ROC)
visaNote: Recipients must obtain a Taiwan Resident Visa/student visa, sponsored through the host university.

title: Australian Volunteers Program - Volunteer Assignments in Bhutan
description: DFAT-funded international volunteering program placing skilled Australian and New Zealand volunteers with partner organizations in Bhutan, mainly in health and education sectors, on assignments ranging from several months to about two years. The program covers international flights, insurance, and a living/accommodation allowance for the assignment period.
org: Australian Volunteers Program (Australian Government Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade)
visaNote: Bhutanese entry/work authorization is arranged with program support; volunteers must be Australian citizens/permanent residents or NZ citizens holding an Australian Special Category Visa.

title: Marine Biology Internship - Marine Savers Maldives
description: Hands-on marine conservation internship based at a Four Seasons Resort marine lab in the Maldives, working with resident marine biologists on coral propagation, reef monitoring, and manta ray/whale shark research. The 70-90 day placement is unpaid but includes free shared accommodation, three meals a day, and internal transfers between the resort and Male; aimed at postgraduate students and early-career marine science candidates.
org: Marine Savers (Four Seasons Resorts Maldives marine conservation program)
visaNote: Maldives entry permit/visa arrangements for the internship period are coordinated by the resort.

title: Rome Prize Fellowship - American Academy in Rome
description: Annual fellowship enabling artists and scholars to live and work at the American Academy in Rome for a full academic year or a half-year term, pursuing independent projects in fields such as archaeology, architecture, literature, visual arts, and history. Full-term fellows receive a $30,000 stipend and half-term fellows $16,000, plus housing and study/studio space at the Academy's Rome campus.
visaNote: Fellows receive support obtaining an Italian long-stay visa for the residency period.

title: Fulbright U.S. Student Program: Open Study/Research Award in Spain
description: Grant for U.S. citizens to pursue independent research, enroll in a graduate program, or combine study and research in Spain for one academic year. Grant benefits include a monthly stipend of approximately EUR1,378, a one-time EUR1,100 international travel allowance, and a EUR200 relocation allowance.
visaNote: Grantees obtain a Spanish student/researcher visa with support from the Fulbright Commission.

title: Fulbright/FLAD Open Study/Research Award in Portugal
description: Grant for U.S. citizens to conduct independent research, enroll in a graduate program, or combine study and research in Portugal for one academic year. Co-sponsored with the Luso-American Development Foundation (FLAD), the award provides a maintenance allowance of about EUR1,300 per month across 8 installments plus a EUR900 one-time international travel allowance.
org: Fulbright Commission Portugal, with Luso-American Development Foundation (FLAD)
visaNote: Grantees obtain a Portuguese student/researcher visa with support from the Fulbright Commission Portugal.
